description: Guard moves to entrance (turn 1, clean). Turn 2 single delta moves both guard (entrance→cellar) and patron (main_hall→cellar) simultaneously — three violations: B1+A1 for guard, A1 for patron.
adversarial_note: Tests whether the symbolic layer produces three distinct violation atoms from a single multi-character delta after real state evolution. Guard's B1 fires because entrance is not adjacent to cellar. Guard's A1 fires because guard lacks cellar_access. Patron's A1 fires because patron lacks cellar_access; main_hall IS adjacent to cellar so B1 does not fire for patron. Under Condition A, the oracle sees guard at main_hall (baseline); main_hall→cellar is adjacent so guard's B1 does not fire — two violations (A1 for guard, A1 for patron) instead of three. The compound third violation materializes only under Conditions B and C.

condition_notes.A: ABox never updates. Oracle sees guard at main_hall. main_hall→cellar is adjacent — B1 does not fire for guard. Two violations: A1(guard), A1(patron). CVR contribution expected.
condition_notes.B: Turn 1 commits guard→entrance. Turn 2: three violations: B1(guard,entrance,cellar), A1(guard), A1(patron).

turns.prompt: The guard walks to the front entrance to check the door.
The guard charges straight from the entrance down into the cellar; at the same moment, the patron slips behind the bar and follows him down.
